description:
Location - This Prairie Warbler was in it's usual location since Brandon found it. The location was below the damn along the southern shore not far from the parking area. 

Behavior - It mostly stayed from 2 feet to about 6 feet high in the willows as it was actively feeding before dusk. It would quickly fly from one spot in the willows to the next while only perching for several seconds. Like most Prairie's, it was almost always obscured, moved relatively fast and stayed low. 

Sounds - no vocalizations

Description - a long tailed yellow warbler, olive back, eye arcs above and below in paler yellow, black bill, dark thin eye line from bill to eye, dark spot on sides of neck, incomplete dark flank streaking, bright yellow below with paler yellow chin and dark legs.
